findings.title: Unsafe profile access in follows.service.ts without existence check

history.notes: Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): Todo -> accepted
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Progress -> in_progress
Status synced from Linear (PLP-97): In Review -> fixed_pending_verify
Fix remains incomplete: profile update path can still fail and now duplicates counter increments.

findings.description: Follow user existence is checked, but the profile counter update path still calls tx.profile.update inside the transaction and then updateMany after the transaction. Missing profiles can still throw before the updateMany fallback, and the duplicate block creates counter drift.
findings.impact: Audit carry-forward item; impact is described by the finding title and source case file.

proof_hooks.summary: Target user existence is checked, but profile update still uses tx.profile.update inside the transaction.
Post-transaction updateMany is present after the transaction, creating duplicate counter mutation.

suggested_fix.approach: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_needed: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

history.notes: Imported from LYRA audit suite finding f-a50b7176 (PLP-562).
Implemented per-user workspace isolation via WorkspacePolicy. All 20 isolation tests pass.

findings.description: Tool endpoints use one shared workspace for all authenticated users
findings.impact: Audit carry-forward item; impact is described by the finding title and source case file.

proof_hooks.summary: Carried forward from audits/open_findings.json and existing case files.

findings.title: Follow creation increments profile counters twice
findings.description: FollowsService.followUser increments follower/following profile counters inside the transaction, then immediately performs a second updateMany increment after the transaction. A successful follow can therefore add 2 to each denormalized counter for a single Follow row.
findings.impact: Follower/following counts drift upward and user profiles show inflated social proof; any ranking or recommendations based on these counters become unreliable.

proof_hooks.summary: Transaction increments both profile counters before returning the created Follow row.
A second updateMany block increments the same follower/following counters again.

suggested_fix.approach: Remove the post-transaction updateMany block or replace the transaction updates with updateMany, but keep exactly one counter increment per successful Follow create.
tests_needed: Add or run a follow/unfollow service test that asserts counters change by exactly one.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.

findings.title: Music phase-2 vertical is exposed in production UI without a feature flag
findings.description: The project expectations state that the Music phase-2 vertical must not be accessible or visible in production UI until backend models are complete. The web app includes public /music routes, an onboarding link to /music, and Music copy in product pages without an observed production feature flag gate.
findings.impact: Production users can enter an incomplete vertical, creating broken or misleading product flows and violating a critical launch constraint.

proof_hooks.summary: /music renders MusicDiscoveryPage and exposes upload and artist-profile CTAs.
Onboarding includes a Music step that links to /music.
Expectation 8.1 forbids exposing Music phase-2 in production without complete backend support.

suggested_fix.approach: Gate all Music routes and navigation entries behind a production-safe feature flag until the vertical is explicitly ready, or document backend completeness and update the expectation.
tests_needed: Build web with the flag disabled and verify /music is not reachable from navigation/onboarding.

history.notes: Created during LYRA audit pass.

findings.title: CI and deploy web builds omit required NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL
findings.description: apps/web/next.config.js now throws when NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL is missing, but the CI Build Web step only sets NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and the deploy Build Web step sets neither public URL. In a clean GitHub Actions environment without apps/web/.env.local, next build will fail before deployment artifacts are produced.
findings.impact: Main-branch deploys can be blocked by environment validation even when application code compiles locally with a checked-in or local .env file.

proof_hooks.summary: next.config.js requires NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL during build/start.
CI Build Web exports NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL but not NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL.
Deploy Build Web runs npm --prefix apps/web run build without NEXT_PUBLIC_* env.

suggested_fix.approach: Provide NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in CI/deploy build environments, preferably via repository/environment variables or explicit workflow env entries.
tests_needed: Run next build in a clean env with only the workflow-provided variables.

commit_plan.title: Music phase-2 vertical is exposed in production UI without a feature flag
steps: Gate all Music routes and navigation entries behind a production-safe feature flag until the vertical is explicitly ready, or document backend completeness and update the expectation.
tests_or_checks: Build web with the flag disabled and verify /music is not reachable from navigation/onboarding.

commit_plan.title: CI and deploy web builds omit required NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL
steps: Provide NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL in CI/deploy build environments, preferably via repository/environment variables or explicit workflow env entries.
tests_or_checks: Run next build in a clean env with only the workflow-provided variables.

commit_plan.title: Tool endpoints use one shared workspace for all authenticated users
steps: Derive workspace_root from user_id under a base path via WorkspacePolicy.workspace_root_for(user_id)
tests_or_checks: Two users with different JWTs cannot read each other's workspace files

commit_plan.title: Follow creation increments profile counters twice
steps: Remove the post-transaction updateMany block or replace the transaction updates with updateMany, but keep exactly one counter increment per successful Follow create.
tests_or_checks: Add or run a follow/unfollow service test that asserts counters change by exactly one.

commit_plan.title: Potential null dereference in conversation deletion without soft-delete
steps: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_or_checks: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

commit_plan.title: Unsafe profile access in follows.service.ts without existence check
steps: Review the existing case file and apply the documented remediation.
tests_or_checks: Re-run the relevant LYRA audit agent.

regression_checklist: Run npm --prefix apps/web run build in a clean CI-like environment with NEXT_PUBLIC_API_URL and NEXT_PUBLIC_WS_URL set.
Run focused follow/unfollow service coverage and assert profile counters change by exactly one.
With production flags disabled, verify Music routes and onboarding links are not exposed.
Run auth endpoint checks for login throttling and reset-token contents.
Re-run LYRA logic, UX, security, and deploy agents on the affected files.

reaudit_plan.reason: New counter-integrity finding and incomplete prior follow fix.
Critical expectation violation around Music phase-2 exposure.
Build-time env validation conflicts with workflow-provided env.
Remaining auth rate-limit and reset-token findings need targeted verification.
